reasoning: The baseline statistical probability from the FRED tool gave a 46% chance of the 5-year yield increasing between March 15 and March 22. However, this period directly overlaps with the March 17-18 FOMC meeting. Recent news as of mid-March indicates rising oil prices due to geopolitical conflict (a US-Iran war mentioned in news reports) and subsequent inflation fears, which have been driving Treasury yields higher in recent days (jumping from 3.73 on March 10 to 3.87 on March 13). Because the Fed is widely expected to hold rates and potentially release a hawkish Summary of Economic Projections (SEP) pushing back rate cut dates due to this new inflationary pressure, there is an elevated probability that the 5-year yield will continue its short-term upward trajectory after the FOMC meeting. Considering the statistical model alongside these strong qualitative drivers, the probability of an increase by March 22 is assessed at 55%.
